discoverer: 2000, JINR Dubna / Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory collaboration — confirmed 2012
etymology: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory — the US national laboratory in Livermore, California, that supplied the plutonium and curium target materials for the synthesis of flerovium, livermorium, moscovium, and oganesson, and whose scientists co-authored the discovery papers. Named in honor of the laboratory's essential contribution.

timeline.event: First synthesis of element 116 (livermorium) reported. confirmed 2012
Longest-lived known isotope: 293Lv, t½ = 53 milliseconds. Individual atoms detected by DGFRS.
IUPAC formally confirms the discovery and approves the name 'livermorium' and symbol 'Li'. Element 116 officially joins the periodic table.

Livermorium was produced by bombarding curium-248 with calcium-48 — the curium target supplied by Lawrence Livermore from US nuclear material stockpiles. It is predicted to be a heavier polonium analog in Group 16. The very short half-life prevents any chemical characterization. Livermorium is the second element named after a US national laboratory (the first being lawrencium, named after Ernest Lawrence).

Research continues at JINR Dubna, GSI/FAIR, RIKEN, and other facilities. Element 116 represents the current frontier of nuclear synthesis — beyond it lies terra incognita, where nuclear stability predictions become increasingly uncertain and experimental access requires advances in accelerator technology and target production.

story: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory was founded in 1952 by Ernest Lawrence and Edward Teller as a second nuclear weapons laboratory (alongside Los Alamos). Its scientists' role in the superheavy element program — providing target materials unavailable elsewhere — earned it an element named in its honor. The laboratory that helped build hydrogen bombs had an element named after it from hydrogen bomb test debris.
source_note: Discovered by Oganessian group (2000) at JINR Dubna in collaboration with Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory. Named after LLNL. Confirmed by IUPAC 2012.
